briefing.headline: Go over the top — spawn clear water sets up explosive topwater action.
briefing.gameplan:
In spawn with clear water and pre frontal conditions, topwater presentations shine.

Wheeler: Use monofilament (Sufix Siege) for topwater fishing — the stretch prevents jerking the bait away from the fish on the hookset. Pull into the fish rather than jerking, letting the sharp VMC hooks get into the fish

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use monofilament (Sufix Siege) for topwater fishing — the stretch prevents jerking the bait away from the fish on the hookset. Pull into the fish rather than jerking, letting the sharp VMC ho
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Pitch a buzzbait like a jig right next to laydowns, under bushes, and skip it under docks so it clacks against the cover; bass in close quarters are used to something dropping and sinking, so
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Johnston: Uses Garmin LiveScope Perspective Mode to spot individual bed fish in rippled conditions when you can't see them visually. Cast to them and catch on first attempt.

Wheeler: Smallmouth spawn starts in creeks and flat areas with no current, then they transition toward the main river/lake — same as largemouth. Look for spawners in 10-15 ft rather than the obvious 4-8 ft most people target

Best for pitching to isolated cover - docks, logs, laydowns, boulders, stumps. A little tough to sink into thick cover. Effective on both smallmouth and largemouth, spawning, cruising, and fry guarding fish
Hackney: 'At Stick Marsh with no underwater vegetation, fish were spawning around willow trees and cane on the shoreline - they just wanted to have something to spawn around.'

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal spawning bass are active on beds in 2-5ft. Falling pressure often triggers new waves of fish to move onto beds. Target hard-bottom spawning flats, secondary pockets, and protected coves.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize lures and switch to brighter colors
If fish are blowing up but not connecting, pause 3-5 seconds after each strike before resuming
